query:
Research the company "campbellsoupcompany".

Research the prospect company for: 1) Recent financial performance gaps—specifically any quarterly misses, lower-than-expected results despite strong functional KPIs, or pulled/revised guidance in the last 6-12 months; 2) Recent CEO transitions or announcement of a strategic review; 3) Current business scale (Fortune 500 status, revenue size, market cap); 4) Industry/business model and how many interdependent functions drive growth (media, production, pricing, distribution, promotions, forecasting); 5) Recent announcements around production expansion, capacity changes, pricing strategy changes, or platform/product launches; 6) Any press releases or earnings calls mentioning margin recovery, cost optimization, or P&L gap explanations; 7) Public visibility around how the company currently manages cross-functional dependencies or if they use siloed optimization tools.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
The Campbell’s Company (formerly Campbell Soup Company) is currently navigating a period of significant financial distress and operational transition. Over the last six months, the company has suffered consecutive quarterly earnings misses, slashed its fiscal 2026 guidance, and seen its market capitalization fall to $6.46 billion, resulting in its removal from the S&P 500 in June 2026. To combat margin compression driven by tariffs and shifting consumer demand, new CEO Mick Beekhuizen is overseeing a strategic review, a $1.2 billion restructuring plan, and extensive supply chain optimizations aimed at recovering profitability.

---

### 1. Financial Performance Gaps and Revised Guidance
Campbell has experienced severe financial underperformance and downward guidance revisions in the first half of calendar year 2026 (fiscal Q2 and Q3 2026):

*   **Quarterly Misses:** In Q2 FY2026, adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $0.51, missing the analyst consensus of $0.57 by approximately 10.5% [Campbell Soup shares dip after earnings miss, outlook cut | Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/campbell-soup-shares-dip-after-earnings-miss-outlook-cut-4554201). This was followed by a Q3 FY2026 revenue miss, reporting $2.37 billion against a Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.40 billion [Campbell's (CPB) Surpasses Q3 Earnings Estimates | Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/campbells-cpb-surpasses-q3-earnings-122501965.html). 
*   **Deteriorating KPIs:** Q3 FY2026 results revealed a 32% year-over-year decline in adjusted EPS and a 4% drop in net sales [The Campbell's Company (CPB) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CPB/). U.S. soup sales fell 8% in Q3, driven by declines in condensed and ready-to-serve products [Campbell's posts Q3 2026 results, keeps outlook | CPB 8-K Filing](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/CPB/8-k-campbell-s-co-reports-material-event-c213835f16d6.html). The Snacks segment margin also dropped 390 basis points quarter-over-quarter to roughly 7% in Q2 FY2026 [Campbell Soup Company (CPB) Earnings Dates, Call Summary & Reports - TipRanks.com](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/cpb/earnings).
*   **Slashed FY2026 Guidance:** 
    *   Full-year adjusted EPS was lowered to $2.15–$2.25, down from $2.40–$2.55 [Campbell Soup shares fall on earnings miss, downwardly revised full-year guidance | NYSE:CPB](https://www.proactiveinvestors.com/companies/news/1088732/campbell-soup-shares-fall-on-earnings-miss-downwardly-revised-full-year-guidance-1088732.html).
    *   Organic net sales are now forecast to decline by 2% to 1%, compared to previous guidance of a 1% decline to 1% growth [Campbell Soup shares fall on earnings miss, downwardly revised full-year guidance | Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/campbell-soup-shares-fall-earnings-142400894.html).
    *   Adjusted EBIT is expected to fall by 20% to 17%, a steeper drop than the previously forecasted 13% to 9% decline [Campbell Soup shares dip after earnings miss, outlook cut | Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/campbell-soup-shares-dip-after-earnings-miss-outlook-cut-4554201).
*   **P&L Gap Explanations:** Management cited a roughly 230-basis-point hit to margins due to gross tariffs on essential materials like steel and aluminum [Tariffs and Tepid Demand: Campbell Soup Slashes Guidance Following Q2 Earnings Miss | FinancialContent](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/marketminute-2026-3-11-tariffs-and-tepid-demand-campbell-soup-slashes-guidance-following-q2-earnings-miss). Additionally, January 2026 weather disruptions caused shipment delays that reduced Q2 net sales by ~1% and adjusted EBIT by $14 million [Campbell Soup shares dip after earnings miss, outlook cut | Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/campbell-soup-shares-dip-after-earnings-miss-outlook-cut-4554201).

### 2. Leadership Transitions and Strategic Review
To address shifting consumer tastes and declining sales, the company is currently undergoing a formal strategic review [Campbell Soup CEO Out, Strategic Review Under Way | Food Manufacturing](https://www.foodmanufacturing.com/home/news/21099526/campbell-soup-ceo-out-strategic-review-under-way). This coincides with a major leadership overhaul:
*   **CEO Transition:** Mick Beekhuizen succeeded Mark Clouse as President, CEO, and company director effective February 1, 2025, following Clouse's retirement to join the NFL’s Washington Commanders [The Campbell’s Company names Mick Beekhuizen as CEO | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/campbells-company-names-mick-beekhuizen-as-ceo/734523/); [The Campbell's Company elects new CEO | Snack Food & Wholesale Bakery](https://www.snackandbakery.com/articles/112644-the-campbells-company-elects-new-ceo).
*   **Transformation Office:** In November 2024, Daniel L. Poland was promoted to the newly created role of Executive Vice President and Chief Enterprise Transformation Officer, with Cassandra Green appointed as SVP and Head of Supply Chain reporting directly to him [Campbell Soup reshuffles leadership, boosts transformation efforts By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/campbell-soup-reshuffles-leadership-boosts-transformation-efforts-93CH-3724339).
*   **Rebranding:** The company officially changed its name from "Campbell Soup Company" to "The Campbell's Company" in November 2024 [The Campbell's Company (CPB) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CPB/).

### 3. Current Business Scale and Market Position
Campbell's market valuation has contracted significantly amid its recent financial struggles:
*   **Market Cap & Index Status:** As of June 2026, the company's market capitalization sits at approximately $6.46 billion, a steep drop from $8.01 billion in February 2026 and $12.49 billion in January 2025 [Campbell Soup Stock Price Today | NASDAQ: CPB Live - Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/equities/campbell-soup); [Campbell Soup (CPB) Market Cap Today: Live Data & Historical Trends](https://public.com/stocks/cpb/market-cap). Consequently, the company was removed from the S&P 500 index on June 5, 2026 [CPB: Campbell's Co - Stock Price, Quote and News - CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/CPB). Following the Q2 2026 earnings miss, the stock hit a 52-week low of ~$21 [Campbell Soup Co. stock hits 52-week low on NASDAQ after Q2 earnings miss and slashed guidance](https://www.ad-hoc-news.de/boerse/news/ueberblick/campbell-soup-co-stock-hits-52-week-low-on-nasdaq-after-q2-earnings-miss/68990648).
*   **Revenue & Headcount:** The company generated ~$10.3 billion in net sales in FY2025 (a 6% YoY increase) and employs approximately 13,700 people [Breaking Down Campbell Soup Company (CPB): Key Insights for Investors – DCFmodeling.com](https://dcfmodeling.com/blogs/health/cpb-financial-health); [Campbell Soup Stock Price Today | NASDAQ: CPB Live - Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/equities/campbell-soup). 
*   **Fortune 500:** Despite recent struggles, the company has maintained its Fortune 500 status every year since 1955 [Campbell's Wants to Make Canned Soup a Hot Commodity Again](https://www.nbcphiladelphia.com/news/local/campbells-canned-soup/2221119/).

### 4. Business Model and Cross-Functional Dependencies
Campbell operates 23 U.S. manufacturing plants and distributes to over 120 countries, holding a dominant 58% to 60% market share in condensed soup [THE CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY - IU ScholarWorks](https://scholarworks.iu.edu/journals/index.php/jsriue/article/download/40082/42417/108777); [Campbell Soup Company (CPB) Business & Moat Analysis | KoalaGains](https://koalagains.com/stocks/NASDAQ/CPB/business-and-moat). Growth is driven by highly interdependent functions:
*   **Distribution & Pricing:** The company uses a dual distribution model: direct-store-delivery (DSD) for bakery items and warehouse delivery for snacks/shelf-stable products [How Does Campbell Soup Company Work? – PortersFiveForce.com](https://portersfiveforce.com/blogs/how-it-works/campbellsoupcompany). Pricing relies on a "pack-price architecture" spanning single-serve to club multipacks, while maintaining a 20% to 25% price premium over generic soup brands [Campbell Soup Company (CPB) Business & Moat Analysis | KoalaGains](https://koalagains.com/stocks/NASDAQ/CPB/business-and-moat); [Strategic Management Report For The Campbell Soup Company Marketing Essay | UKEssays.com](https://www.ukessays.com/essays/marketing/strategic-management-report-for-the-campbell-soup-company-marketing-essay.php).
*   **Cross-Functional Forecasting:** Campbell actively manages cross-functional data dependencies. By blending point-of-sale (POS) data, promotional schedules, and weather signals, the company increased forecast accuracy from 68% to 86% and reduced stockouts by 22% [Campbell Soup Saves $53M in Supply Chain Case Study](https://blog.gettransport.com/sk/learn-how-campbells-soup-saved-53m-in-supply-chain-management-case-study/). Furthermore, its Continuous Replenishment (CR) program reduced retailer inventories by 66% while maintaining ~99% fill rates [CAMPBELL SOUP'S CONTINUOUS REPLENISHMENT PROGRAM: EVALUATION AND ENHANCED INVENTORY DECISION RULES - GfiRARD CACHON, MARSHALL FISHER, 1997](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1111/j.1937-5956.1997.tb00430.x).
*   **Media & Digital:** The company integrates media with product discovery, investing $185.8 million in advertising (FY2023) and operating a digital recipe platform that drives 3.6 million monthly views [Marketing Mix Analysis of Campbell Soup Company (CPB) – DCFmodeling.com](https://dcfmodeling.com/products/cpb-marketing-mix); [Campbell Soup Company (CPB): Business Model Canvas – DCFmodeling.com](https://dcfmodeling.com/products/cpb-business-model-canvas).

### 5. Margin Recovery, Cost Optimization, and Capacity Changes
To combat shrinking margins and P&L gaps, Campbell has initiated aggressive cost optimization and portfolio reshaping:
*   **Restructuring & Savings:** The company announced a $1.2 billion restructuring plan aimed at boosting margins [Campbell Soup's High Yield: A Trap or a Value Opportunity Amid Deteriorating Fundamentals?](https://www.ainvest.com/news/campbell-soup-high-yield-trap-opportunity-deteriorating-fundamentals-2512/). It is targeting ~$250 million in annual savings by FY2028 through supply chain optimizations, which includes closing its Tualatin, Oregon plant [How Does Campbell Soup Company Work? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/how-it-works/campbellsoupcompany).
*   **Production Expansion:** Campbell is investing $230 million in capital projects through FY2026 to optimize its network, including a $150 million allocation for new aseptic soup production in Maxton, North Carolina [Campbell Soup: Supply Chain Optimization Plan to Fuel Growth](https://blog.foodshippers.org/campbell-soup-supply-chain-optimization-plan-to-fuel-growth); [Campbell Soup Invests $230M into Supply Chain | CareersInFood.com](https://m.careersinfood.com/career-planning/exploring-food-beverage/industry-news/united-states/campbell-soup-invests-230m-into-supply-chain).
*   **SKU Rationalization:** The company recently removed 12% of its low-velocity SKUs, generating $15 million in savings and increasing margin per pallet by 3.2% [Campbell Soup Saves $53M in Supply Chain Case Study](https://blog.gettransport.com/sk/learn-how-campbells-soup-saved-53m-in-supply-chain-management-case-study/).
*   **Portfolio Shifts:** Following its $2.7 billion acquisition of Sovos Brands (Rao's) in March 2024, Campbell announced in December 2024 it would acquire a 49% stake in Rao's partner La Regina SPA for $286 million [How Does Campbell Soup Company Work? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/how-it-works/campbellsoupcompany); [Campbell's keeps annual forecasts intact as shoppers rein in spending | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/campbells-keeps-annual-forecasts-intact-as-shoppers-rein-in-spending-2025-12-09/). Conversely, it divested its noosa yoghurt business, completing the sale on February 24, 2025 [Campbell's Reports Second Quarter Fiscal 2026 Results - The Campbell's Company](https://www.thecampbellscompany.com/newsroom/news/campbells-reports-second-quarter-fiscal-2026-results/).

---

### Gaps and Limitations
*   **Siloed Optimization Tools:** The provided research highlights successful cross-functional data integration (e.g., blending POS, weather, and promotions), but there is no specific mention of whether the company currently struggles with or utilizes "siloed optimization tools."
*   **Q1 FY2026 Discrepancy:** There is conflicting data regarding Q1 FY2026 EPS performance; one source indicates a miss by 8.0% ($0.65), while another claims a beat ($0.77). 
*   **Specific Details of the Strategic Review:** While a strategic review was announced alongside the CEO transition, specific proposed outcomes or timelines for this review were not detailed in the provided findings.
